title: T-066 — setPin/verifyPin caller-contract JSDoc + pool.end() lifecycle fix (FT-006)
prompt:
# T-066 — setPin/verifyPin caller-contract JSDoc + pool.end() lifecycle fix (FT-006)

PRD: `docs/prd/00-FOUNDATION-PRD.md` — sections: §5.x
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`
Original carry-forward ID: **FT-006** (from `_meta/audits/2026-05-07-foundation-final-carry-forward-backlog.md`)
Severity: **P1** (S effort)

## Goal
Refactor verify-pin to use a shared pool with closeAuthPool lifecycle and add caller-contract JSDoc to setPin/verifyPin.

## Implementation contract
1. Module-level `let pool: Pool | null = null` + lazy initialiser `getAuthPool()`.
2. Export `closeAuthPool(): Promise<void>` for graceful shutdown.
3. Remove all `pool.end()` calls from setPin/verifyPin bodies.
4. JSDoc above each export: argument expectations, return shape, error contract, thread-safety statement.
5. Test asserts pool is reused across two consecutive verifyPin calls (same client reference).

## Files (scope)
- packages/auth/src/verify-pin.ts [modify]
- packages/auth/src/__tests__/verify-pin-lifecycle.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given a verifyPin call followed by another verifyPin call, when both run, then they share the same underlying Pool instance (pool === pool, verified by exposed test hook).
2. Given `closeAuthPool()` is called, when a subsequent verifyPin runs, then a fresh pool is created.
3. Given setPin/verifyPin in TypeScript, when hovered in an IDE, then JSDoc describes argon2id expectation + thread-safety + error codes.

## Test strategy
- RED: write verify-pin-lifecycle.test.ts asserting pool reuse + closeAuthPool re-init before refactor.
- Run `pnpm --filter @monopilot/auth test verify-pin-lifecycle`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not store the plaintext PIN anywhere (still argon2id).
- Do not leak the pool on test teardown — closeAuthPool must run in vitest afterAll.
- Do not use raw new pg.Pool — use the app-role helper from T-058.

## Closeout evidence
Provide: changed_files, test_commands_and_results, acceptance_criteria_status (per AC), deviations_from_prd, git_status. Use the test-driven-development skill (RED-first) and requesting-code-review at GREEN.

pipeline_inputs.description: Add caller-contract JSDoc to setPin/verifyPin and replace per-call pool.end() with shared lifecycle (closeAuthPool helper).
pipeline_inputs.details: T-016 currently calls `pool.end()` after every setPin/verifyPin invocation — kills connection reuse and ruins p95. Replace with module-level pool + exported `closeAuthPool()` for graceful shutdown. Add JSDoc spelling out: caller must pass argon2id-hashed PIN (never plaintext), pool ownership semantics, and concurrent-safe guarantees.
out_of_scope: Do not touch RLS on user_pins (FT-002).
